headline: Sosma Section 4 challenged in Federal Court test case
context: A landmark Federal Court case in 2025 challenges the constitutionality of SOSMA Section 4's 28-day detention without judicial review.

cards.big: Federal Court hears constitutional challenge to SOSMA Section 4 in 2025
cards.sub: Test case could determine whether 28-day detention violates Article 5

cards.big: SOSMA Section 4 allows 28-day detention with no requirement for judicial authorisation
cards.sub: Only initial arrest requires reasonable suspicion under the Act

cards.big: Article 5 of Federal Constitution guarantees personal liberty to all persons
cards.sub: Question is whether security exception in Article 149 overrides Article 5 protections

cards.big: Challenge brought by family of 26-year-old detainee held for 56 days in 2024
cards.sub: Young man detained twice under SOSMA Section 4 without being charged

cards.big: 56 days of detention without charge is the constitutional question made personal
9-member Federal Court bench convened, largest since Semenyih Jaya 2017 case
